findings.evidence: `rg` shows `rfl` proofs at `HopfBaseChange.lean:67`, `:73`, `:80`, `:104`, `:116`; Mathlib provides the corresponding named lemmas at `Mathlib/RingTheory/HopfAlgebra/TensorProduct.lean:73`, `Mathlib/RingTheory/Coalgebra/TensorProduct.lean:64,69`, and `Mathlib/RingTheory/TensorProduct/Basic.lean:786,789`.
findings.fix: Prove them via the named Mathlib lemmas, e.g. `TensorProduct.antipode_def`, `Coalgebra.TensorProduct.counit_tmul`, `Coalgebra.TensorProduct.comul_tmul`, `AlgHom.liftEquiv_tmul`, and `AlgHom.liftEquiv_symm_apply`, using `simpa`/`rw` after unfolding only the local abbreviation if needed.
findings.issue: The base-change formula lemmas at lines 67, 73, 80, 104, and 116 close by `rfl`, relying on definitional equality through tensor-product Hopf/coalgebra and `AlgHom.liftEquiv` wrappers.

summary: Several public wrapper lemmas are proved by `rfl` through Mathlib tensor-product implementation details even though named Mathlib lemmas exist for the same facts. Replace these with proofs that cite the named API so this file is robust to upstream refactors.
